title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
sub: The Temple of Speed: the fastest circuit on the calendar. Long straights and big braking into chicanes, run at minimum downforce, where slipstreaming and late lunges define the race.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: The main straight into the Turn 1-2 Rettifilo chicane (huge braking + the main passing spot), then flat-out through Curva Grande to the Roggia chicane.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The two quick Lesmo rights, the run under the old banking, and the back straight into the Ascari chicane.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The fast Ascari chicane, the long straight, and the legendary Parabolica (Curva Alboreto) sweeping onto the line.

pit: one of the longer pit lanes
tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: Very low downforce means little cornering load, so degradation is among the lowest of the year. A straightforward one-stop dominates unless a safety car shuffles the order.
spots: End of the main straight + DRS, the heaviest braking zone on the lap. The classic Monza lunge.
A late dive into the second chicane after the Curva Grande slipstream.
Slipstream-assisted moves into the Ascari chicane and a tow onto the line.
otNote: Huge slipstreams down the long straights make Monza a passing playground. Late braking into the Rettifilo chicane is the defining overtaking move of the year.
corners: Massive stop from ~360 km/h off the main straight + DRS. The prime overtaking spot.
Long, fast right taken near flat after the chicane.
Left-right chicane; a late-braking dive after the Curva Grande tow.
First of the two quick, committed Lesmo rights.
Tighter second Lesmo onto the run under the old banking.
Flat-out kink on the back straight toward Ascari.
A quick left-right-left flick; rhythm and commitment are everything.
The legendary Parabolica: a long, tightening right onto the main straight.
The start/finish straight back to the Rettifilo.
geo: The fastest circuit on the calendar, ~360 km/h top speeds and ~80% of the lap at full throttle. Teams run their skinniest, lowest-drag wings of the year.
Set in the Monza royal park since 1922, one of the oldest purpose-built circuits in the world. The crumbling old banking still looms in the trees.
Ferrari’s home race and a sea of red. A win here is the most emotional on the calendar for the Scuderia.
Few corners and minimal lateral load mean tyre wear is among the lowest of the season, so it’s a near-guaranteed one-stop.
